parsed_review.summary: CP00-529 is a descriptor-only closeout pack for RP17 AI Governance (P04 workflow permission slice): 40 units, RP17.P04.M03.S19-RP17.P04.M05.S14, risk class B. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level claims are supported. (1) Distributions: the pack binding (registry.js:256-271) and requirements (registry.js:1407-1456) match the baseline; I recounted the 40 included_units and they reconcile to deliverable {claude_review:4,implementation:11,ui:18,security_audit:4,fixture:1,test:1,hermes_evidence:1} and micro-title {Primary:4,Secondary:22,Permission:14}. (2) Implementation: all 7 required public exports exist (service.js:1973,2006; validators.js:2827,2862,2918,2939,2962), descriptor/case-set build on CP528 upstream with every row descriptor_only:true/runtime_execution:false, and the package source is pure with no fs/fetch/child_process/DB/network usage. (3) Contract/validator: contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json current_pack and p04_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor projection match code, and validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs deepEquals contract against code plus asserts plan presence. (4) Tests/commands: 3 CP529 tests plus command-evidence.json record npm test, npm run validate, all rpNN validates, closeout-pack-plan:validate, weighted/spec/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00 control-plane, and git diff --check all passing (exit 0). (5) Authority boundary: claude_is_final_approval:false, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true, human_final_approval_required, and Hermes used only as a deterministic evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). (6) Review hardening: forbidden_review_evidence rejects auth failures, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, and missing fields; readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1444 bytes, valid JSON). (7) production_ready is correctly false/implemented_pending_review; production_ready_candidate:true is a local candidate gated behind human approval. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook, ldip) are correctly preserved and excluded from pack scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one informational P3 notes that post-review artifacts are not yet present, which is the expected pre-review state. Claude is acting strictly as a read-only independent reviewer and is not the final approver; the human final-approval / enterprise-trust gate remains closed outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review closeout artifacts not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: The manifest required_pack_artifacts and per-unit evidence_refs reference docs/closeout-packs/cp00-529/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, none of which exist at review time (only manifest.json and command-evidence.json are present). pack_level_claude_review.status is 'pending' with empty valid_review_receipts. This is the correct pre-review state per the hardened_review_sequence (baseline -> readiness -> read-only run -> normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation -> commit); it is not a defect in the implementation slice and does not affect the descriptor-only code, tests, contract, or boundary attestations.
findings.recommendation: No action required for the implementation slice. Ensure the post-review pipeline produces claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json from the accepted review and that npm run closeout-pack:validate gates on their presence before commit, so production_ready is only asserted after human final closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; expected pre-review state. Non-blocking for pack and goal closeout. Final closeout validation should confirm these artifacts are generated before commit.
